summary: Plan's architectural premise is verified end-to-end: detect_sanitizer_ast IS dead code at L3490 with zero callers; both regex dispatch sites at L4101 (process_block) and L4350 (ssa_propagate) are confirmed; TaintInfo.sanitized_vars exists at L244; the WALK-ONCE-INDEX-BY-LINE pattern at L3565-3606 exists exactly as described; all 16 AST sanitizer banks are non-empty; compute_taint at L3993 internally parses + delegates to compute_taint_with_tree (so AST path is reachable from both entry points). HOWEVER three CONDITIONAL issues block clean /autonomous dispatch: (1) BLOCKER — M3's two pre-flagged 'parity gaps' are ALREADY FILLED at HEAD (Ruby Rack::Utils.escape_html at taint.rs:2416, PHP (int)/(float) at taint.rs:2717). M3 collapses to a no-op audit and the plan should be amended to acknowledge this. (2) BLOCKER — the M4 obsolete-test deletion list is INCOMPLETE: the plan enumerates only 'test_<lang>_detect_sanitizers' patterns (16 confirmed in taint_tests.rs L973-1321 — actual count 17, not 13-16) but MISSES 3 tests in taint_tests.rs (test_int_sanitizes_sql_injection L322, test_shlex_quote_sanitizes_command_injection L335, test_html_escape_sanitizes_xss L347) that use is_sanitizer + find_sanitizers_in_statement with non-empty assertions, AND 4 tests in tests/security_tests.rs (test_detect_sanitizer_python_int L383, test_detect_sanitizer_python_shlex L390, test_detect_sanitizer_python_html_escape L397, test_detect_sanitizer_typescript L671) that the plan does not list at all under M4 deletions. Without these, cargo test --workspace will FAIL deterministically at the M4 atomic gate. The field_access_info-extension-v1 premortem caught the EXACT same shape (6 vs 3 tests). The plan's M1 enumeration mandate (gating in reports/M1-test-enumeration.json) is the right escape hatch but the plan's text gives an under-counted estimate that will mislead the executor. (3) BLOCKER — plan §2 M1 RED-first harness specifies a non-existent function compute_taint_with_tree_with_patterns(...) and calls it 'pub(crate) test-only seam; same shape as field_access_info M1 added'. Field_access_info M1 actually added an AST_ONLY_TEST_MODE thread-local Cell + AstOnlyTestModeGuard RAII type that short-circuits detect_sources/detect_sinks ONLY — NOT detect_sanitizer. So the M1 harness as written cannot be implemented; the executor must either (a) extend the AST_ONLY_TEST_MODE check to detect_sanitizer (a small but real source change in M1, not test-only as plan implies), or (b) add a different harness shape. Recommendation: amend plan §2 M1 to specify (a) explicitly. None of these BLOCK indefinitely — all are amendable in <50 LOC of plan-text changes — but they BLOCK clean dispatch as-written.

tigers.claim: detect_sanitizer_ast at taint.rs:3490 with ZERO call sites
tigers.verification: grep + Read of taint.rs L3490 + grep -rn detect_sanitizer_ast crates/ --include=*.rs
tigers.evidence: taint.rs:3490 declares pub fn detect_sanitizer_ast; only references are L36-37 doc comment and L3490 definition itself; zero invocations in compute_taint_with_tree, process_block, ssa_propagate, or any test file outside the to-be-added M1 fixtures
tigers.implication: Wiring premise is correct. No reframe required.

tigers.claim: TaintInfo has sanitized_vars: HashSet<String> populated by both call sites today
tigers.verification: Read of taint.rs L232-269 (TaintInfo struct + Default impl) + L4102/L4352 dispatch sites
tigers.evidence: L244 declares pub sanitized_vars: HashSet<String>; L4102 sanitized_vars.insert(var_ref.name.clone()); L4352 sanitized_vars.insert(v); both write into TaintInfo.sanitized_vars via &mut
tigers.implication: No TaintInfo / TaintFlow API extension required. Plan claim correct.

tigers.claim: All 16 AST sanitizer banks are non-empty (handoff '5 zero-content' was FALSIFIED)
tigers.verification: grep + Read of each *_AST_SANITIZERS bank L1730 (Python), L1985 (TS), L2062 (Go), L2135 (Java), L2203 (Rust), L2262 (C), L2308 (Cpp), L2406 (Ruby), L2466 (Kotlin), L2514 (Swift), L2580 (CSharp), L2639 (Scala), L2713 (PHP), L2769 (Lua), L2821 (Elixir), L2891 (OCaml)
tigers.evidence: 16 banks; all have ≥1 AstSanitizerPattern entry. Spot-checked 5: Lua L2769 has call_names: ['tonumber']; OCaml L2891 has call_names: ['int_of_string', 'float_of_string']; Rust L2203 has 8 raw-substring entries; Kotlin L2466 has 4 wildcard member_patterns; Ruby L2406 has 2 entries (Numeric to_i/to_f + Html CGI/Rack)

tigers.claim: M3 needs to ADD raw-fallback entries for Ruby Rack::Utils.escape_html and PHP (int)/(float)
tigers.verification: Read of RUBY_AST_SANITIZERS at L2406-2420 and PHP_AST_SANITIZERS at L2713-2730
tigers.evidence: RUBY_AST_SANITIZERS L2416 ALREADY contains member_patterns: [("", "CGI.escapeHTML"), ("", "Rack::Utils.escape_html")]. PHP_AST_SANITIZERS L2717 ALREADY contains member_patterns: [("", "(int)"), ("", "(float)")] with comment '`(int)`, `(float)` are cast expressions — raw fallback.'. Both 'gaps' identified by the planning worker are already filled at HEAD e69eed3.
tigers.implication: M3 is a no-op audit at HEAD. The 2 pre-flagged tests (ruby_rack_utils_escape_html_sanitizer_truncates_flow_ast_only, php_int_cast_sanitizer_truncates_flow_ast_only) should transition RED → GREEN at M2 (with wiring alone), not M3. M3's actual purpose collapses to: 'verify all 16 ast_only tests are GREEN; surface any unforeseen gap from the 7 verify-during-M1 langs'. The plan + dispatch contract overstate M3's scope.

tigers.claim: WALK-ONCE-INDEX-BY-LINE pattern at L3565-3606 exists exactly as described
tigers.evidence: L3565-3606 walks tree.root_node() ONCE via detect_sources_ast(&root, src, language, None) and detect_sinks_ast(&root, src, language, None); indexes via HashMap<u32, Vec<TaintSource>> and HashMap<u32, Vec<TaintSink>> by line; comment at L3567-3568 documents 'walk the tree ONCE (no line filter) to avoid O(lines * nodes) quadratic slowdown that caused infinite-loop-like hangs'
tigers.implication: Architecture exists; M2 helper build_sanitizer_ast_index is a clean mirror. R1 mitigation is sound.

tigers.claim: compute_taint at L3993 was refactored to internal-parse-and-delegate; AST path always reachable
tigers.evidence: L4027-4036 reconstructs source from statements, calls crate::ast::parser::parse, then on Ok(tree) delegates to compute_taint_with_tree(..., Some(&tree), Some(src.as_bytes()), language, None). On Err(_) returns Ok(TaintInfo::default()).
tigers.implication: M4's 'compute_taint without tree' fallback (_ => false) at the AST-only flip is dead-in-practice. test_compute_taint_sanitizer_removes_taint at security_tests.rs:509 will continue to work because compute_taint internally parses + delegates.

tigers.claim: process_block signature at L4061 + SsaPropagateCtx at L4174 — private API, single-caller refactor
tigers.evidence: process_block fn signature has 8 params, all private (called only from compute_taint_with_tree at L3757). SsaPropagateCtx is a private struct constructed only in compute_taint_with_tree. M2 extending both with sanitizer_ast_index parameter is a strictly-internal change.
tigers.implication: R5 (internal API extension) is LOW severity. Plan correct.

elephants.risk: M3 parity-fill claim is FALSE at HEAD; M3 is a no-op audit
elephants.blast_radius: Plan + dispatch contract mislead executor; M3 commit becomes a 0-LOC change OR a redundant addition that clippy may flag as duplicate
elephants.mitigation_in_plan: PARTIAL — plan §0 reframe acknowledges 'parity-AUDIT not parity-FILL', but plan §2 M3 still specifies the 2 raw-fallback ADDs as if they were missing. Plan §3 Ruby (L362) and §3 PHP (L393) text says 'GAP' and 'M3 ADD' explicitly, contradicting the actual file state.
elephants.recommended_amendment: Amend plan §2 M3 + dispatch-contract.json M3.green_files.additions to: 'VERIFY raw-fallback entries exist at L2416 (Ruby) and L2717 (PHP); add ONLY if M1 parity audit surfaces an unforeseen gap from the 7 verify-during-M1 langs. Expected M3 LOC delta: 0-12 (was estimated +12 unconditional).' Update §0 'two confirmed gap candidates' to 'two PRE-FILLED entries; possible unforeseen gaps from the 7 verify-during-M1 langs only.'

elephants.risk: M4 obsolete-test deletion list is incomplete; cargo test fails at M4 atomic gate
elephants.blast_radius: M4 atomic commit FAILS — cargo test --workspace returns non-zero; rollback rule triggers full revert; one cycle wasted
elephants.mitigation_in_plan: PARTIAL — plan + dispatch contract have a gating mandate `m1_test_enumeration_required` saying 'M1 must enumerate all tests touching sanitizer Vec path' and 'gates M4 deletion list'. This is the correct escape hatch IF the M1 executor enumerates exhaustively, NOT just the test_<lang>_detect_sanitizers pattern. But the plan's prose UNDERCOUNTS at '13-16' and lists ONLY the test_<lang>_detect_sanitizers shape, which will bias the executor.
elephants.recommended_amendment: Amend plan §2 M4 deletions list AND §4 stop-thresholds to enumerate three additional categories explicitly: (a) `test_int_sanitizes_sql_injection` at taint_tests.rs:322, `test_shlex_quote_sanitizes_command_injection` at L335, `test_html_escape_sanitizes_xss` at L347 — these use is_sanitizer + find_sanitizers_in_statement on Python and assert non-empty. (b) `test_detect_sanitizer_python_int` (security_tests.rs:383), `test_detect_sanitizer_python_shlex` (L390), `test_detect_sanitizer_python_html_escape` (L397), `test_detect_sanitizer_typescript` (L671) — these call detect_sanitizer directly and assert Some(SanitizerType::*). (c) `test_compute_taint_sanitizer_removes_taint` (security_tests.rs:509) is OK to KEEP because it uses compute_taint which internally parses + delegates to the AST path. Total accurate count: 16-17 (taint_tests.rs `test_<lang>_detect_sanitizers`) + 3 (taint_tests.rs is_sanitizer/find_sanitizers triplet) + 4 (security_tests.rs detect_sanitizer triplet+1) = 23-24 tests to delete OR adapt. Plan's '13-16' estimate is off by ~30-50%.

elephants.risk: M1 RED harness uses non-existent function compute_taint_with_tree_with_patterns
elephants.blast_radius: M1 executor cannot implement the harness as specified; either (a) invents the missing public API (a public-API change disallowed by mandate `public_api_unchanged`) or (b) deviates from the plan and chooses a different harness shape
elephants.mitigation_in_plan: NOT ADDRESSED — plan §2 M1 (L178-184) explicitly references compute_taint_with_tree_with_patterns and claims 'same shape as field_access_info M1 added'. The actual field_access_info M1 added an AST_ONLY_TEST_MODE thread-local + AstOnlyTestModeGuard RAII type at taint.rs:51-83 that short-circuits ONLY detect_sources / detect_sinks (taint.rs:816-818, 933-935 etc) — NOT detect_sanitizer (L1096-1104 has no AST_ONLY_TEST_MODE check).
elephants.recommended_amendment: Amend plan §2 M1 RED-first harness to specify the actual harness shape: 'EXTEND AST_ONLY_TEST_MODE check to detect_sanitizer (taint.rs:1096) — a 3-line addition mirroring detect_sources at L816-818. AstOnlyTestModeGuard then short-circuits all three regex paths (sources, sinks, sanitizers). The harness analyze_sanitize_ast_only is a one-liner: { let _g = AstOnlyTestModeGuard::new(); analyze(src, lang, fn_name) } where analyze is the existing helper at common/integ_helpers.rs:44.' This is a SOURCE change in M1 (adds 3 LOC to taint.rs) — bumps M1 LOC delta from 200 to 203 and means M1 is no-longer-test-only. Update dispatch-contract M1.green_files to include taint.rs anchor.

elephants.risk: Per-language verdict verify_during_M1 may flush more parity gaps than expected from the 7 langs
elephants.blast_radius: M3 LOC delta grows beyond +12; minor — plan already has the carry_forward_exceptions mandate
elephants.mitigation_in_plan: ADDRESSED — plan §2 M3 conditional clause + plan §9 carry-forward protocol + plan §10 mandate 4

blockers.description: Plan §2 M3 + dispatch-contract.json M3 specify ADDING raw-fallback entries for Ruby Rack::Utils.escape_html and PHP (int)/(float) — but BOTH are ALREADY PRESENT at HEAD e69eed3 (taint.rs:2416 and taint.rs:2717 respectively). M3 collapses to a no-op audit unless the 7 verify-during-M1 langs surface unforeseen gaps. Plan + dispatch contract overstate M3's scope and would mislead the executor into adding duplicate entries (which clippy may flag).
blockers.amendment_required: Amend plan §2 M3 narrative + dispatch-contract.json M3.green_files.additions to remove the Ruby/PHP add-instructions and replace with VERIFY-only language. Update plan §3 Ruby (L362-363) and §3 PHP (L393) to reflect that the entries ARE present, with verbatim text referencing taint.rs:2416 and taint.rs:2717. Update §0 'Implication for scope' table to remove '2 raw-fallback entries' from M3 expected output (becomes 'MAYBE 0-2 unforeseen entries from the 7 verify-during-M1 langs').

blockers.description: M4 obsolete-test enumeration is incomplete in the plan prose. Plan §2 M4 (L273-282) enumerates only `test_<lang>_detect_sanitizers` patterns and estimates 13-16 tests — but actual count is 17 in taint_tests.rs PLUS 3 additional sanitizer tests (test_int_sanitizes_sql_injection L322, test_shlex_quote_sanitizes_command_injection L335, test_html_escape_sanitizes_xss L347) that use is_sanitizer + find_sanitizers_in_statement on Python with non-empty assertions, PLUS 4 tests in tests/security_tests.rs (test_detect_sanitizer_python_int L383, test_detect_sanitizer_python_shlex L390, test_detect_sanitizer_python_html_escape L397, test_detect_sanitizer_typescript L671) that call detect_sanitizer directly. Total deletion target: 17 + 3 + 4 = 24 tests, NOT 13-16. Without amending, M4 atomic commit will fail cargo test --workspace.
blockers.amendment_required: Amend plan §2 M4 'Deletions in `taint_tests.rs`' bullet to add the 3 Python-named-shape tests (L322, L335, L347) and a new bullet 'Deletions in `crates/tldr-core/tests/security_tests.rs`' for the 4 tests at L383, L390, L397, L671. Update obsolete count estimate from '13-16' to '23-24'. Update CHANGELOG draft §6 'Removed' bullet count similarly. Update dispatch-contract.json M4.green_files for taint_tests.rs to mention the 3 named tests, and ADD a third green_files entry for tests/security_tests.rs with the 4 tests. Pre-emptively add to M1.additional_artifacts the requirement that M1-test-enumeration.json covers BOTH files and ALL three patterns (test_<lang>_detect_sanitizers, is_sanitizer-asserting, detect_sanitizer-direct-asserting). The plan's gating mandate m1_test_enumeration_required is correct in principle but the prose estimate biases the executor.

blockers.description: Plan §2 M1 RED-first harness specifies a function compute_taint_with_tree_with_patterns that DOES NOT EXIST at HEAD e69eed3 and was NOT added by field_access_info-extension-v1 M1 (which actually added AST_ONLY_TEST_MODE thread-local + AstOnlyTestModeGuard RAII type at taint.rs:51-83 that short-circuits detect_sources/detect_sinks ONLY, not detect_sanitizer). The M1 executor cannot implement the harness as written. They will either (a) invent a new public function (violates `public_api_unchanged` mandate), (b) silently use a different shape and risk diverging from the spec.
blockers.amendment_required: Amend plan §2 M1 RED-first harness section (around L162-191) to specify the ACTUAL harness shape: 'M1 ADDS a 3-line short-circuit to detect_sanitizer at taint.rs:1096 mirroring detect_sources at L816-818 — `if AST_ONLY_TEST_MODE.with(|m| m.get()) { return None; }`. AstOnlyTestModeGuard then short-circuits sources, sinks, AND sanitizers uniformly. analyze_sanitize_ast_only becomes: `{ let _g = AstOnlyTestModeGuard::new(); common::integ_helpers::analyze(src, lang, fn_name) }`'. Update M1 LOC delta from 200 to 203. Update M1 green_files to include taint.rs anchor (3 LOC). Update §10 mandate `no_taint_info_extension` adjacent to clarify that this 3-line short-circuit is not an API extension (it is internal flag-checking on the existing AST_ONLY_TEST_MODE Cell). Note that M4 atomic deletion does NOT need to remove this short-circuit (it stays for any future test harness use).

non_blocking_observations.description: Plan §0 reframe (handoff '5 zero-content banks' falsified) is correct AND well-documented. The reconciliation pointing to regex-removal-v1/reports/worker-2-integ-tests.json:530 'ZERO member_patterns and 100% call_names' is plausible. This is good investigation discipline.
The atomic-commit boundary for M4 is justified and parallels regex-removal-v1 W2-atomic + field_access_info-extension-v1 M5. Plan §7 trade-off analysis is clear.

WALK-ONCE-INDEX-BY-LINE mandate (R1) is the MOST important architectural constraint. Without it, the milestone risks reintroducing the L3567-3568 historical infinite-loop hang. The mandate is correctly elevated to validator_mandates and §10 mandate 5.

M2's AST-FIRST-WITH-REGEX-FALLBACK gate during the migration window is correctly motivated. The post-M4 collapse to AST-only is mechanically clean because regex Vecs are empty and detect_sanitizer returns None deterministically.

The 16 RED string-literal-substring regression-guard tests are the BAKE-IN for the closes-#24-shaped FP class for sanitizers. Plan §4 'String-literal substring regression guards' correctly notes that pre-M2 some langs FAIL (regex incorrectly matches the substring) and ALL must PASS post-M4 (regex deleted, AST is_in_string filter active). Good adversarial test design.

test_compute_taint_sanitizer_removes_taint at security_tests.rs:509 is SAFE to keep — it uses compute_taint which internally parses + delegates to compute_taint_with_tree (per L4027-4036). The AST sanitizer path will fire for `safe_id = int(user_id)` at Python because Python AST has call_names: ["int"]. No deletion needed.
test_sanitizer_type_serialization at security_tests.rs:695 is SAFE to keep — it serializes SanitizerType enum, not the regex bank. Unaffected by Vec emptying.

specific_validations_performed.claim_detect_sanitizer_ast_at_L3490: VERIFIED — taint.rs:3490 declares pub fn detect_sanitizer_ast(root: &tree_sitter::Node, source: &[u8], language: Language, line: u32) -> Option<SanitizerType>
specific_validations_performed.claim_zero_call_sites: VERIFIED — grep finds zero invocations of detect_sanitizer_ast outside the function definition. The only references are doc comments at L36-37 and the definition site itself. mod.rs exports it but no caller.
specific_validations_performed.claim_TaintInfo_has_sanitized_vars: VERIFIED — taint.rs:244 declares `pub sanitized_vars: HashSet<String>`. L4102 and L4352 both write into it via &mut.
specific_validations_performed.claim_all_16_banks_non_empty: VERIFIED. Spot-checked 5 of 16: PYTHON L1730 (3 entries), RUBY L2406 (2 entries with Rack::Utils raw-fallback ALREADY present), KOTLIN L2466 (4 wildcard member_patterns), LUA L2769 (1 entry call_names: ['tonumber']), OCAML L2891 (1 entry call_names: ['int_of_string', 'float_of_string']). Plan §0 falsification of '5 zero-content' is correct. Plan's per-bank entry count table at §0 matches the file state.
specific_validations_performed.claim_M3_parity_targets_2_entries_only: REFUTED — both pre-flagged 'gaps' are ALREADY filled at HEAD. RUBY_AST_SANITIZERS L2416 has member_patterns: [("", "CGI.escapeHTML"), ("", "Rack::Utils.escape_html")]; PHP_AST_SANITIZERS L2717 has member_patterns: [("", "(int)"), ("", "(float)")] with comment '`(int)`, `(float)` are cast expressions — raw fallback.'. M3 should be a no-op audit UNLESS the 7 verify-during-M1 langs surface unforeseen gaps.
specific_validations_performed.claim_walk_once_pattern_at_L3565_3606: VERIFIED — taint.rs:3565-3606 walks tree.root_node() ONCE for sources + sinks; indexes via HashMap<u32, Vec<TaintSource>> by line. Comment at L3567-3568 documents the historical infinite-loop hang. Pattern is exactly as plan describes; build_sanitizer_ast_index is a clean mirror.
specific_validations_performed.obsolete_test_enumeration_complete: INCOMPLETE in plan prose. Actual enumeration: (a) 17 test_<lang>_detect_sanitizers tests in taint_tests.rs at L973 (typescript), L1000 (javascript), L1017 (go), L1044 (java), L1061 (rust), L1078 (c), L1101 (cpp), L1124 (ruby), L1151 (kotlin), L1173 (swift), L1198 (csharp), L1223 (scala), L1248 (php), L1278 (lua), L1290 (luau), L1303 (elixir), L1321 (ocaml). Plan estimated 13-16 — actual is 17. (b) THREE Python-named-shape tests in taint_tests.rs at L322 test_int_sanitizes_sql_injection, L335 test_shlex_quote_sanitizes_command_injection, L347 test_html_escape_sanitizes_xss — these use is_sanitizer + find_sanitizers_in_statement and assert non-empty. NOT in plan's deletion list. (c) FOUR detect_sanitizer-direct tests in tests/security_tests.rs at L383 test_detect_sanitizer_python_int, L390 test_detect_sanitizer_python_shlex, L397 test_detect_sanitizer_python_html_escape, L671 test_detect_sanitizer_typescript. NOT in plan's deletion list (plan only mentions taint_tests.rs deletions in M4). Total: 24 tests. Plan estimate is 30-50% under. Plan §6 CHANGELOG bullet count needs updating. test_compute_taint_sanitizer_removes_taint (security_tests.rs:509) and test_sanitizer_type_serialization (L695) are SAFE to keep.
specific_validations_performed.public_api_callers_audit: Sanitizer functions (detect_sanitizer, is_sanitizer, find_sanitizers_in_statement) are called externally ONLY from crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint_tests.rs and crates/tldr-core/tests/security_tests.rs. NO production code outside taint.rs uses them. After M4 they become safe no-ops because their iteration over patterns.sanitizers terminates immediately on empty Vec. Mandate `public_api_unchanged` is achievable WITHOUT signature changes.

risk_register.risk: M1 RED harness compute_taint_with_tree_with_patterns is fictional
risk_register.blast_radius: M1 commit fails to compile or executor invents a wrong-shape harness
risk_register.mitigation: BLOCKER-3: amend plan §2 M1 to specify AST_ONLY_TEST_MODE short-circuit extension to detect_sanitizer (3 LOC in taint.rs)

risk_register.risk: M4 atomic commit fails cargo test due to undercounted obsolete-test enumeration
risk_register.blast_radius: Full M4 revert per rollback rule; one cycle wasted
risk_register.mitigation: BLOCKER-2: amend plan §2 M4 deletions to enumerate the 3 Python-named-shape + 4 security_tests tests; mandate M1 enumeration covers BOTH files and ALL three patterns

risk_register.risk: M3 specifies adding entries that are already present (duplicate addition)
risk_register.blast_radius: Either an executor adds duplicates (clippy may flag) or correctly recognizes M3 is a no-op and creates an empty commit (mandate `wire_dispatch_before_delete` doesn't strictly forbid empty M3 commits, but it's wasteful)
risk_register.mitigation: BLOCKER-1: amend plan §2 M3 to verify-only language; expected M3 LOC delta becomes 0-12 conditional

risk_register.risk: Per-line walk pattern in build_sanitizer_ast_index reintroduces O(N²) hang
risk_register.blast_radius: Test suite hangs / OOMs on large files
risk_register.mitigation: Plan §10 mandate 5 (`walk_once_index_pattern`) is correctly elevated. R1 in plan §8 explicit. As long as executor follows §5 helper spec, mitigated.

risk_register.risk: find_sanitizers_in_statement / is_sanitizer become no-ops; clippy flags dead Vec iteration
risk_register.blast_radius: M4 stop threshold cargo clippy fails
risk_register.mitigation: Iteration over an empty Vec is valid Rust; clippy does not flag. R7 in plan §8 covers this.

risk_register.risk: Verify-during-M1 surfaces a gap that no AST or raw-fallback shape can address

risk_register.mitigation: Plan §9 carry-forward protocol + §10 mandate 4 + dispatch-contract validator_mandate `carry_forward_exceptions_documented`

risk_register.risk: AST_ONLY_TEST_MODE thread-local interaction with parallel cargo test execution
risk_register.blast_radius: Test flakiness if multiple sanitizer tests run in same thread without guards
risk_register.mitigation: AST_ONLY_TEST_MODE is thread-local (per taint.rs:51 `Cell::new(false)` in thread_local! macro). RAII guard restores previous value on Drop. Field_access_info M1 used the same pattern without flakiness. LOW severity.

wave_safety_assessment.M1_red_test_scaffold: INADEQUATE as written due to BLOCKER-3 (fictional API). Otherwise sound — the 50 test functions, dual-path RED protocol, and parity-audit reports are well-specified. Once the harness shape is amended to use AST_ONLY_TEST_MODE extension, M1 is solid.
wave_safety_assessment.M2_dispatch_wiring_safety: ADEQUATE. The walk-once helper spec at plan §5 is well-formed; threading via private parameter to process_block + private field on SsaPropagateCtx is single-caller; both call sites (L4101 and L4350) are explicitly enumerated; AST-FIRST-WITH-REGEX-FALLBACK gate is behavior-preserving during the migration window. R1 (per-line walk pattern) is elevated to a validator mandate, which is correct.
wave_safety_assessment.M3_parity_fill_safety: REFRAMED — actual M3 is a verify-only audit at HEAD. The 2 pre-flagged gaps are already filled. Plan + dispatch contract overstate M3's scope (BLOCKER-1). Once amended, M3 becomes a clean conditional no-op (or 0-2 LOC if 7 verify-during-M1 langs surface unforeseen gaps).
wave_safety_assessment.M4_atomic_commit_safety: INADEQUATE deletion list (BLOCKER-2). The 30 regex tuple deletions across 16 banks are correctly enumerated. The dispatch-flip at L4101/L4350 is straightforward. But the obsolete-test deletion list undercounts by 7 tests (3 Python is_sanitizer/find_sanitizers + 4 security_tests detect_sanitizer). Once amended, M4 atomicity is achievable. Rollback rule (full revert) is correctly specified.
wave_safety_assessment.M5_changelog_tag_safety: ADEQUATE. CHANGELOG draft at plan §6 is well-formed; needs minor count update for 'Removed' bullet (24 tests, not 13-16) once BLOCKER-2 amendment lands. Local-tag-only protocol matches predecessor pattern.

discriminative_tests_recommended.name: test_python_int_in_string_inside_function_call_does_not_sanitize
discriminative_tests_recommended.purpose: Stress the is_in_string filter — verify a sanitizer call appearing as a substring INSIDE another call's string argument is correctly ignored. E.g., `print("use int(x) to convert"); eval(raw)` — `raw` must remain tainted because `int(x)` is inside a string literal, NOT a real call.
discriminative_tests_recommended.fixture:
def f():
    raw = input('> ')
    print("use int(x) to convert")
    eval(raw)
# Expected post-M4: ≥1 TaintFlow (raw is tainted; the string-literal int(x) does NOT sanitize)

discriminative_tests_recommended.name: test_php_int_cast_with_extra_whitespace_does_not_falsely_match
discriminative_tests_recommended.purpose: Verify the raw-substring `(int)` match handles tree-sitter-php's tokenization of `( int )` with whitespace. The raw-substring fallback uses node text containment; if extract_call_name returns `(int)` without whitespace but the tree node spans `( int )`, parity may fail.
discriminative_tests_recommended.fixture:
<?php
$safe = ( int ) $_GET['x'];  // tree-sitter-php cast_expression with whitespace
eval($safe);
# Expected post-M3+M4: 0 TaintFlow (cast_expression detected via raw-substring '(int)' — verify match works on node TEXT not regex pattern)

discriminative_tests_recommended.name: test_ruby_rack_utils_with_module_alias_still_sanitizes
discriminative_tests_recommended.purpose: Verify the raw-substring match `Rack::Utils.escape_html` works on aliased forms — e.g., `Utils = Rack::Utils; Utils.escape_html(x)`. This is a known LIMITATION of raw-substring; if it fails, document as a carry-forward exception.
discriminative_tests_recommended.fixture:
Utils = Rack::Utils
def f
  raw = STDIN.gets
  safe = Utils.escape_html(raw)
  eval(safe)
end
# Expected: probably 1 TaintFlow (alias not detected) — DOCUMENT as known limitation, NOT a bug

discriminative_tests_recommended.name: test_lua_tonumber_inside_table_constructor_sanitizes
discriminative_tests_recommended.purpose: Verify the call_names path fires for `tonumber` even when nested inside a table-constructor or function-argument context, not just at top-level assignment.
discriminative_tests_recommended.fixture:
local raw = io.read()
local t = { safe = tonumber(raw) }
os.execute(t.safe)
# Expected post-M4: 0 TaintFlow IF call_names path matches descendants; 1 TaintFlow if it only matches direct assignments
